Facts.text: Owain Glyndŵr is declared Prince of Wales by his followers
King Johan Casimir of Palts promises military aid to the Huguenots
Albert VII Archduke of Austria's Spanish forces lose control of the city of Amiens to French King Henry VI
Massachusetts village of Shawmut changes its name to Boston
Spanish troops occupy Dunkirk
Russian troops occupy Smolensk in Poland
Jewish mystic and rabbi Sabbatai Zevi, the "Messiah," appears before Sultan Mehmed IV, puts on a Turkish turban and converts to Islam
Polish King John II Casimir Vasa resigns and goes to France the following year
Holy Roman Emperor Leopold I declares war on France, Cologne, and Bavaria
William IV Prince of Orange installed as Stadtholder of Groningen
French troops occupy Bergen op Zoom in the Netherlands
Great Seal of the United States of America used for the first time
British capture Cape Town, South Africa, from the Dutch
Oliver Wendell Holmes writes poem "Old Ironsides" as tribute to the 18th-century USS Constitution
Slavery is abolished in all French territories

Federal Constitution of the United Mexican States of 1857 comes into force, establishing individual rights, including universal male suffrage and freedom of speech. Also removes Catholicism as the official religion and is thus fiercely attacked by Pope Pius IX.
First overland mail to California
British Post Office Savings Banks opens
Confederate General Braxton Bragg's army surrounds 4,000 federals at Munfordville, Kentucky
Robert College of Istanbul, Turkey, the first American educational institution outside the United States, is founded by Christopher Robert, an American philanthropist.
US Civil War: Battle of Coggin's Point, Virginia (Hampton-Rosser Cattle Raid)
Ottawa Rough Riders & Senators play Canadian Football game
Golf's first recorded hole-in-one is scored by Tom Morris at Prestwick's 8th hole in Scotland
German troops leave France
5th America's Cup: Puritan (NY Yacht Club) beats Genesta (Royal Yacht Squadron, Isle of Wight, UK) by 1:38 for 2-0 series win
Amsterdam swimming club renamed "The Y"
Cherokee Strip, Oklahoma, opens white settlement homesteaders
Alturas, California, is incorporated as the only city in Modoc County
Kaarlo Nieminen wins 1st Finnish marathon
Carriage-maker, William C. Durant, founds General Motors in Flint, Michigan
Thousands of women demonstrate for Dutch female suffrage
US takes control of the customs and finances of Haiti for ten years
American Legion incorporated by an act of US Congress
Dutch Ruether beats Giants 4-3 to clinch Cincinnati 1st NL pennant
-22] Hurricane in Florida & Alabama, kills 372
Italian-Romanian peace treaty signed
Philip Dunning & George Abbott's play "Broadway" premieres in NYC
St Louis Cards beat Phillies 23-3
San Felipe Segundo/Okeechobee hurricane crosses through the Bahamas as a category 4 storm, killing 18, as it travels from Puerto Rico to Florida
Police shoot at strikers in Maastricht, 2 killed
Phillies trailing 10-5, score 5 in 9th, then Pirates score 4 in top of 10th, so Phillies score 5 in bottom of 10th to win 15-14
Blimp is moored to Empire State Building (NYC)
St Louis Cards repeat as NL champions with a 6-3 win over Phillies
30.8 cm rainfall at Westerly, Rhode Island (state record)
British racing driver George Eyston sets world auto speed record at 357.5 MPH
NY Yankees clinch their 11th & 4th successive pennant
Luftwaffe attacks central London
Samuel Rayburn of Texas elected speaker of US House of Representatives
St Louis Browns Johnny Lucadello is 2nd to HR from each side of plate
German armoured troops surround Kyiv, Ukraine
Jews of Vilna, Poland, confined to the Ghetto
Japanese attack on Port Moresby, Papua New Guinea, repelled
Montgomery's 8th army contacts invasion - arm forces at Salerno
Soviet army under general Vatutin reconquers Romny, Northern Ukraine
Barometric pressure at 856 mb (25.55") off Okinawa (record low)
John Cobb sets world auto speed record at 394.2 MPH
Typhoon Kathleen hits Saitama, Tokyo and Tone River area, killing at least 1,930
KABC TV channel 7 in Los Angeles, CA (ABC) begins broadcasting
Cleveland Browns (formerly AAFC) play 1st NFL game, beat Philadelphia 35-10)
Viet Minh offensive against French bases in Vietnam
US National League umpire Frank Dascoli clears the Dodgers bench, ejecting 15 players
AL approves St Louis Browns move to become Baltimore Orioles
CKLW TV channel 9 in Windsor, ON (CBC) begins broadcasting
Bauer & Berra homer in 9th beating Red Sox 5-4 taking over 1st
US Auto Club forms to oversee four major auto racing categories
YankeeMickey Mantlepulls a hamstring muscle running out a bunt
Coup in Thailand  deposes Premier Songgram
Los Angeles City Council approves a 300-acre site in Chavez Ravine for the Dodgers
Amos Alonzo Stagg retires as a football coach at 98
Milwaukee Braves' future HOF pitcherWarren Spahnno-hits Philadelphia Phillies, 4-0 at County Stadium
CDU loses West German election
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
WLKY TV channel 32 in Louisville, Kentucky (ABC) begins broadcasting
Brian Kilby wins marathon: (2:23:18.8)
Gerda Kroon runs European record 800 m in 2:02.8
Public TV channel 13 begins in NYC
USSR performs nuclear test at Novaya Zemlya USSR
"The Outer Limits" premieres on ABC-TV
WVAN TV channel 9 in Savannah, GA (PBS) begins broadcasting
"Shindig" premieres on ABC-TV
Boston Red Sox Dave Morehead no-hits Cleveland Indians, 2-0
Sobibor trial opens in Hagen, West Germany
Anni Pede runs female world record marathon (3:07:26)
KPAZ TV channel 21 in Phoenix, AZ (IND) begins broadcasting
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
KLNI (now KADN) TV channel 15 in Lafayette, LA (IND) begins
King Hussein of Jordan forms military government
6 Ku Klux Klansmen arrested in connection with bombing of 10 school buses
A number of Unionists resign over the proposed tripartite talks involving Northern Ireland, the UK, and the Republic of Ireland
1st TV series about mixed marriage, "Bridget Loves Bernie" premieres
Bay Area Rapid Transit (BART) begins regular rail service crossing under the San Francisco Bay, through the Transbay Tube
MLB Pittsburgh Pirates second baseman Rennie Stennett is second to hit 7-for-7 in a 9-inning MLB game in a 22-0 rout  of Chicago Cubs, Wrigley Field in Chicago
Papua New Guinea gains independence from Australia (National Day)
The first prototype of the MiG-31 interceptor makes its maiden flight.
American Episcopal Church approves ordination of women as priests & bishop
Egyptian president Sadat re-elected
Shavarsh Karapetyan saves 20 people from the trolleybus that had fallen into Erevan reservoir.
90 minute pilot of "Logan's Run" premieres on TV
Seattle beats Royals, 4-1, to end KC's winning streak at 16 games
Sire Records releases "Talking Heads: 77", the debut studio album by the American rock band Talking Heads; features the single "Psycho Killer"
7.4 magnitude earthquake strikes near Tabas, central Iran, killing an estimated 15,000 to 25,000 people
American rockers the Grateful Dead perform last of three-night stand at the Giza Sound and Light Theater beside the Sphinx at the foot of the Great Pyramid in Egypt
New York Yankees beat Boston Red Sox, 3-2; at Yankee Stadium; Blue Bombers 6th win over arch rivals in 2 weeks
"Rapper's Delight", the debut single by hip-hop trio Sugar Hill Gang is released by Sugar Hill Records
Catfish Hunter Day at Yankee Stadium
Coup in Afghanistan under Hafizullah Amin
KC's Willie Wilson hits 5th inside-the-park HR (most since 1925)
Russian Bolshoi Ballet dancers Leonid & Valentina Kozlov defect to US in Los Angeles, California
The families of Peter Strelzyk and Gunter Wetzel arrive in West Germany from Communist East Germany in a hot air balloon
USSR performs nuclear test
Massacre of 1000+ Palestinian refugees at Chatila & Sabra begins
Fire in Kinross gold mine, Transvaal South Africa, 177 killed
Calif's Bob Boone catches record 1,919th major league game
New York City's WNET-TV channel 13 begins round clock broadcasting
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Cincinnati Reds Tom Browning pitches a perfect game, beats LA Dodgers, 1-0
Jury awards Valerie Harper $1.6 M in dispute over TV series
Scottish singer Fish leaves progressive rock group Marillion
Pirate Radio NY International begins transmissions on WWCR
Atlanta's Otis Nixon suspended for rest of 1991 due to cocaine
Norm Charlton suspended for 7 days for intentionally hitting Steve Scioscia with a pitch
"Black Wednesday" UK government is forced to withdraw the pound sterling from the European Exchange Rate Mechanism after unable keep it above agreed lowest limit
900 die in flood in Pakistan
FCC votes to allow competition for local phone service
Fire Department puts out smokey electrical fire in the White House
1st One-Day International in Canada, India vs. Pakistan at Toronto
Howard Stern Radio Show premieres in Panama City, Florida on WTBB 97.7 FM
NASA launches Space Shuttle STS 79 (Atlantis 17)
American Basketball League MVP Nikki McCray signs with the WNBA
14.8 inches (37.6 cm) rainfall from Hurricane Floyd at Myrtle Beach, South Carolina (state record)
1st ever season of "Big Brother" reality show begins on the Veronica channel in The Netherlands
Camorra Mafia boss Paolo Di Lauro is arrested in Naples
One-Two-GO Airlines Flight 269, carrying 128 crew members and passengers, crashes in Phuket, Thailand, killing 89 people
14 people are killed and 7 wounded by a roadside bomb Jandol, Turkey
8 female civilians are killed by a NATO airstrike in Laghman, Afghanistan
8 police officers are killed by a roadside bombing by Kurdistan Workers' Party militants in Turkey
Anti-Japanese protesters set fire to Panasonic plant in Qingdao, China
Mayor of Chicago, Rahm Emanuel, seeks a court order to resolve a week long teachers strike
NHL locks out its players after the expiry of the collective bargaining agreement
12 people are killed after a gunman opens fire at a naval yard in Washington, D.C.
21 people are killed by Hurricane Ingrid in Mexico
700 million malaria cases prevented in Africa since 2000 in report by University of Oxford in "Nature" journal
8.3 magnitude quake hits off coast of Illapel, Chile killing 11 and prompting evacuation of 1 million
Military coup in Burkina Faso, President Michel Kafando and other officials seized by presidential guards
Oil tanker explosion kills 170 in Maridi, South Sudan
Report 3 million people die each year of air pollution, more than malaria and HIV/Aids combined published in "Nature" journal
Second Republican presidential candidates debate, hosted by CNN, Carly Fiorina andDonald Trumpface off at the Reagan Presidential Library
New Zealand All Blacks rugby union team hands South Africa their biggest test match defeat in history; beat Springboks, 57-0 in Rugby Championship round in Albany, NZ
James Francis Edward Stuart (13) "The Old Pretender", becomes the Jacobite claimant to the thrones of England and Scotland on the death of his fatherJames II
Carl Maria von Weber's opera "Silvana", starring his future wife Carone Brandt in the title role, premieres at the Nationaltheater in Frankfurt, Confederation of the Rhine

PriestMiguel Hidalgo y Costillarings the church bells in Dolores, Mexico, issuing a call to arms known as the "Grito de Dolores (Cry of Dolores)", calling for the end of Spanish rule. Celebrated today as Mexican Independence Day.
George Canningbecomes British Foreign Secretary for the second time, serving until 1827
ShakespeareBirthplace Trust buys bard's birthplace in Stratford-upon-Avon
British explorerDavid Livingstoneis the first European to sight Lake Nyasa - now forms Malawi's boundary with Tanzania & Mozambique
Stephen Craneis threatened with arrest after intervening when a woman he was interviewing, Dora Clark, is arrested on suspicion of prostitution
French painterPaul Gauguinsettles in Atuona in the Marquesas Islands
TsarNicholas IIof Russia adjourns 4th Duma
St. Cardinals future Baseball HOF first basemanJim Bottomleysets MLB all-time single game RBI record of 12 in a 17-3 rout of Brooklyn Robins at Ebbets Field
Redskins NFL franchise play first game in Washington at Griffith Stadium; defeat the New York Giants in season opener, 13–3
Leo Durochersuspended from Ebbetts Field for "inciting a riot"
US PresidentFDRsigns Selective Training & Service Act - 1st US peacetime draft
Adolf Hitlerorders that for every dead German, 100 Yugoslavs should be killed
1st movie in Cinemascope "The Robe" based on the book by Lloyd C. Douglas, directed by Henry Koster and starringRichard Burtonand Jean Simmons premieres
French PresidentCharles de Gaullerecognizes Algerian right of self determination
Federation of Malaysiaformed by Malaya, Singapore, British North Borneo (Sabah) and Sarawak
British singer Davie Jones changes his stage name to "David Bowie" to avoid confusion with the Monkee of the same name
Metropolitan Opera House opens in Lincoln Center, New York City, withLeontyne Priceas Cleopatra in "Antony and Cleopatra" bySamuel Barber
Richard Nixonappears on "Rowan & Martin's Laugh-in"
West German chancellorWilly Brandtmeets with Soviet leaderLeonid Brezhnev
"TheBob NewhartShow" sitcom (the Chicago one) debuts on CBS-TV, the first episode "Fly Unfriendly Skies" starsPenny Marshallas The Stewardess
Buffalo running backO.J. Simpsonrushes for a then NFL record 250 yards in the Bills, 31-13 win at New England
Bob Dylanbegins recording his 15th album "Blood on the Tracks", in New York City
US GeneralAlexander Haig, Jr. becomes NATO supreme commander in Europe
US PresidentGerald Fordannounces conditional amnesty for US Vietnam War deserters
Ringo Starrreleases single "Drowning in Sea of Love" in the UK
Filming begins for "Monty Python's Life of Brian", on location in Monastir, Tunisia
Austrian weightlifter and actorArnold Schwarzeneggerbecomes a US citizen
1st broadcast of "Miami Vice" on NBC-TV
PopeJohn Paul II's ends his Los Angeles tour with an interfaith meeting at Dodger Stadium with leaders of Judaism, Islam, Buddhism and Hinduism
14th Toronto International Film Festival: "Roger & Me" directed byMichael Moorewins the People's Choice Award
42nd Emmy Awards: LA Law, Murphy Brown,Peter Falk& Patricia Wettig win
Iraq televises an 8 minute uncensored speech fromGeorge H. W. Bush
US trial of former Panamanian leaderManuel Noriegabegins
"Frasier" starringKelsey Grammerand David Hyde Pierce premieres on NBC
Minnesota TwinsDave Winfieldis 19th to get 3,000 hits
Greg Madduxof Braves sets record of 17 consecutive road victories
Courtroom reality show "Judge Judy" with JudgeJudith Sheindlinpremieres in the US
MLB Minnesota Twins'Paul Molitorbecomes 21st player to reach 3,000 hits
Apple Computer Inc. names co-founderSteve Jobsas interim CEO
Mark McGwiresigns with the St Louis Cardinals for $26M
Sammy Sosabecomes the second player to hit 50 or more home runs in three consecutive years, joiningMark McGwire
Talk show "Dr. Phil" withPhil McGrawand co-created byOprah Winfreydebuts on syndicated US TV
59th Emmy Awards: The Sopranos, 30 Rock, James Spader &Sally Fieldwin
The critically acclaimed "Drive", starringRyan Gosling, is released
Paul Kagamewins re-election to the Rwandan presidency in a landslide
Barbra Streisandreleases album "Partners" features duets with other artists, when reaches No 1, makes Streisand only recording-artist with top album in six decades
Chris Sharmafree solos Alasha in Mallorca, Spain, the world's first 9b (5.15b) deep-water solo route
The Mayflower departs Plymouth, England, with 102 Pilgrims and about 30 crew for the New World [O.S. Sep 6]
The "Wall Street bombing" occurs at 12:01 when a horse-drawn wagon explodes on Wall Street, New York, killing 38 and injuring 143
British actress Peg Entwistle (24) commits suicide by jumping from the letter "H" in the "Hollywoodland" (now "Hollywood") sign in Los Angeles, California
